name: Bobby Durham
abstract: Bobby Durham (February 3, 1937 – July 6, 2008) was an American jazz drummer. Durham was born in Philadelphia and learned to play drums while a child.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald, Singer
JVC Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald, Singer
JVC Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald, Singer

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: I did a record with Bobby during – I think it was during the ’60s... So it was a lot of fun. She wasn’t in the best of health... Sweets [Harry Edison] and Bobby Durham, Ray Brown.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: He used to live out – Bobby Durham. Bobby Durham used to live with him over there. He stayed over there until he died recently. So Bobby used to be with him and give lessons every time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Keter Betts
about_quotes.evidence: Ed left in ‘72 because he had married and his wife had a baby, and they got — Bobby Durham I think it was. Yeah. And so I was Tommy, Tommy and me stayed until ‘76.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keter Betts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Blue
about_quotes.evidence: We were playing at Le Duc de Lombards in Paris and Bobby came to sit in... Bobby was another one of those special beings
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Blue interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 23, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: He stayed there a lot, him and Bobby Durham, the drummer. They were working Ella when I was with Basie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pierre Boussaguet
about_quotes.evidence: Ray Brown, Bobby Durham, and then Freddie Hubbard, Dizzy Gillespie —
about_quotes.doc_title: Pierre Boussaguet interviewed by Matthieu Jaffre, Aix en Provence, France, July 28, 2020
